hooks.prompt: Review the desktop app (Electron/Tauri) code change for issues. Check for: 1) Electron main process with nodeIntegration: true and contextIsolation: false (critical security vulnerability — always use contextIsolation: true and preload scripts with contextBridge), 2) IPC handler without input validation (renderer can send arbitrary data to main — validate all ipcMain.handle arguments with schema validation), 3) Loading remote URLs in BrowserWindow without proper security (XSS to RCE — set webSecurity: true, use Content-Security-Policy, restrict navigation with will-navigate event), 4) Missing auto-update configuration (users stuck on old versions — implement electron-updater or Tauri's built-in updater with code signing), 5) Storing sensitive data in localStorage or electron-store without encryption (accessible on disk — use safeStorage.encryptString() for secrets, keytar for credentials), 6) Missing app protocol handler registration (deep links don't work — register custom protocol with app.setAsDefaultProtocolClient and handle second-instance event), 7) File system access without path validation (path traversal vulnerability — validate and sanitize all file paths, restrict access to app data directory), 8) Main process performing CPU-intensive work (freezes UI — use worker threads, utility process, or move computation to renderer with Web Workers), 9) Missing crash reporter and error logging (bugs unreported — configure crashReporter.start() and implement structured logging with rotation), 10) Packaging without code signing and notarization (macOS Gatekeeper blocks install, Windows SmartScreen warns — sign with developer certificate, notarize for macOS). Return {"ok": true} if no issues, or {"ok": false, "reason": "specific issue found"} if problems detected.
